jcr:content.jcr:description: AbbVie supports employee well-being through resources that aid in mental health, financial health, fitness, nutrition, maintaining health habits.
jcr:content.jcr:title: Wellbeing in the Workplace
jcr:content.ctaAltText: Learn more about well-being in the workplace
jcr:content.description: Support for your physical, mental and financial well-being
jcr:content.title: Wellbeing in the Workplace

text.text: People do their best when they're at their best. That's why we support our employees' physical, mental and financial wellbeing.

To face day-to-day challenges our global Employee Assistance Program (EAP) is here to help. EAP is a free confidential service providing assistance, counseling and coaching on personal or work concerns such as stress, grief, chronic illness, relationship and family concerns and referrals to legal and financial resources. EAP is available 24/7 to employees via phone, website, email or messaging. Employees have 8 to 10 free sessions, depending on location.

We offer webinars for all employees on a variety of mental health wellness topics to ensure you can be your best self, at work and at home. Leaders are also provided learning opportunities to develop the skills and confidence to identify and support employees struggling with mental health challenges and guide them to the right resources.

text_copy.text:
At AbbVie, we understand that our employees’ needs and priorities outside of work change over time. We’re committed to supporting the diverse and evolving needs at every stage of our team’s careers. Our flexible ways of working can help employees work in ways most effective to their individual needs. Flexibility, which varies by role, includes part-time work, job sharing, and hybrid work arrangements for office-based employees.

In addition to statutory leave entitlements, AbbVie offers employees additional leave. This includes three extra days of paid leave per year for AbbVie Appreciation Day (August) and two days of leave between Christmas and New Year during the office closure period. AbbVie also offers two optional days of paid leave for volunteering because it takes all of us to make an impact in our communities.

title_copy_984608474_79221829.jcr:title: Recognise the connection between physical and mental health

text_937322991.text: Your physical and mental health are interconnected. We offer a variety of programs that reduce the stigma around conditions like migraine, menopause and mental health. In doing so, we create space for open dialogue and better access to care, which contributes to stronger individual and team performance.

quote_1661374647.quote: It means a lot to work in a company that's not just talking about wellbeing but truly living it. The fact that we're having open conversations about various "life stage" topics such as menopause - and backing them up with real resources and supports - sends a powerful message. It makes me feel seen, valued and part of a culture that's evolving with its people

title.jcr:title: Take care of the people you love

text.text:
At AbbVie, we help you take time for family, whether you’re welcoming a new child, ensuring your children have great day care and academic success, or looking after ageing loved ones.
No matter your stage in life, our support benefits and programs and generous leave policies help you focus on caring for the people who matter to you.
of paid leave inclusive of all types of families welcoming a child through birth, adoption, surrogacy or fostering

text_copy.text: paid leave to care for family members with a serious health condition

text.text: AbbVie’s flagship wellbeing program is called Vitality. Vitality provides employees with an extensive range of resources to support their wellbeing journey on topics such as exercise, nutrition and sleep. Throughout the year, the Vitality program brings a focus to different topics, often complimented by local events and initiates to ensure employees can be their best self at work and at home.

title_copy_copy.jcr:title: Access to medical services and wellbeing resources

text.text: AbbVie employees across ANZ have access to a range of other great wellbeing resources and second opinion medical services provided through Vivo in Australia and AIA-MyCare in New Zealand

text_copy.text: Regardless of which superannuation fund you decide to choose, AbbVie offers salary continuance, funeral, life and total permanent disability insurance for all employees up to the age of 65. Employees are automatically enrolled into the policy through our provider upon start.

title_copy.jcr:title: Get financial support when it matters most

text.text: When the unexpected happens, you can count on AbbVie to help. The AbbVie Employee Relief Program (AERP) provides short-term assistance with basic living expenses in response to financial hardship during times of major personal hardship or in the event of a disaster. The AERP, which is part of the AbbVie Foundation, also gives support to children of employees by offering scholarships to help them achieve their full potential.
